title: Collapse duplicated `ResultType` annotation handling into one authoritative path
problem: After reviewing `TODO.md`, `implementation_notes.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `Bugs.md`, and the current code, the most worthwhile still-open bounded simplification is inside the diagnostic result-type path. `Bugs.md` has no live open items, and most documented cleanup campaigns are closed, but `src/MLF/Elab/Run/ResultType.hs` and `src/MLF/Elab/Run/ResultType/Fallback.hs` still carry parallel annotation logic: both reimplement the same `ALet`/`AAnn` root-peeling rules, and `src/MLF/Elab/Run/ResultType/Fallback.hs` keeps a private `computeResultTypeFromAnnLocal` that ignores the witness/trace/scope inputs used by `src/MLF/Elab/Run/ResultType/Ann.hs`. That leaves two semantics for annotation-driven result-type reconstruction in a path that is still exercised by annotated lambdas and guarded by tests.
why_this_is_simpler: Make `MLF.Elab.Run.ResultType.Ann.computeResultTypeFromAnn` the single annotation-aware engine, extract one shared helper for the `ALet`/`AAnn` peeling logic, and let `Fallback` handle only genuinely non-annotation roots. This removes duplicate branching, deletes the weaker local annotation handler, and makes the module boundary explicit: `Ann` owns annotation semantics, `Fallback` owns non-annotation diagnostics.
why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: Thesis-exactness should be preserved because annotation translation and elaboration are paper-defined, not convenience-defined: the thesis gives the annotation/coercion story in §12.3.2 and the elaboration path in §15.3.6 and §15.3.8. Even though `src/MLF/Elab/Run/Pipeline.hs` reports the type-checker result as authoritative, the diagnostic result-type path is still used as a checked guardrail, so it should reuse the already thesis-aligned witness/trace/scope logic instead of maintaining a second approximation that can drift.
expected_benefits: Removes one live duplicate implementation of annotation result-type logic.
Reduces branching by making `Fallback` a true non-annotation path.
Lowers drift risk for annotated-lambda diagnostics by reusing the witness/trace-aware path.
Makes the ResultType module split easier to understand and maintain during future row2/result-type cleanup.
risks: Annotated-lambda body handling may currently depend on the simplified recursive behavior, so unification could expose hidden assumptions.
The shared peel helper must preserve the current distinction between canonicalized `annCanon` and pre-redirect `ann`.
Because this path is diagnostics-oriented, regressions may be subtle unless the parity guards remain intact.
required_validation: `cabal test mlf2-test --test-show-details=direct --test-options='--match "AAnn root: primary annotation result type matches fallback facade"'`
`cabal test mlf2-test --test-show-details=direct --test-options='--match "ResultType|Phase 6 — Elaborate|chi-first gate stays green"'`
